title: Vaccinium longipes
taxonomy: Vaccinium longipes is a species of flowering plant that belongs to the family Ericaceae. It is a type of shrub that is native to the mountainous regions of the Americas. The classification of Vaccinium longipes is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Clade: Angiosperms, Clade: Eudicots, Clade: Asterids, Order: Ericales, Family: Ericaceae, Genus: Vaccinium, Species: V. longipes. This classification reflects the plant's evolutionary relationships and its position within the plant kingdom. Further research may be necessary to confirm the accuracy of this classification.
uses: Vaccinium longipes is a plant species found in the mountainous regions of the Pacific Northwest in North America. There is limited information available on the medicinal uses of Vaccinium longipes, but its close relative, Vaccinium membranaceum, is known to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. However, specific medicinal uses of Vaccinium longipes remain largely unexplored. More research is needed to determine the potential health benefits of this plant species. Traditional uses of Vaccinium longipes by indigenous communities have not been extensively documented or studied.

side_effects: Vaccinium longipes, commonly known as the long-stalked blueberry, is native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America.
